Business Content 1: In the state of Maryland (MD), starting from January 22, 2026, individuals must provide an initial notification regarding a notice of claim for disability, critical illness, or rider benefits. This notification must occur within a timeframe of 30 to 365 days from the start of the disability or the triggering event. If it is not possible to meet this deadline, the notification should be made as soon thereafter as is reasonably possible.

Business Content 1: In the state of Maryland, starting from January 22, 2026, for claims related to Proof of Loss, an initial written proof is required. This requirement is triggered at the end of the elimination period or the start of liability. Claimants must submit this proof within 90 to 365 days. This requirement is mandatory for the validation of claims.

Requirement Type=Initial Written Proof
Trigger Event=End of Elimination Period / Start of Liability
Deadline / Timelinebetween90 and 365 Days

Action=Require initial written proof of loss for claim validation.

Business Content 1: In the state of Maryland, effective January 22, 2026, there is a requirement related to the Proof of Loss category. If a claim is submitted late, an extension for submission may be granted if the submission occurs after the 90 to 365 day deadline has expired. This extension allows for an additional period of up to one year for submission, but it is only valid if it can be demonstrated that earlier proof of loss was not possible, except in cases where there was a lack of legal capacity.
Rule 1:
State=MD
Effective Date=2026-01-22
Category=Proof of Loss
Requirement Type=Late Submission Extension
Trigger Event=After the 90–365 day deadline expires
Deadline / Timeline=Up to 1 Year
Condition / Exception=Valid only if earlier proof was not possible (except for lack of legal capacity).
Action=Grant late submission extension for proof of loss claims.
